THE
ICE

7 expertly prepared ice tracks in Hallingdal. Norway's premier professional driver training arena for over 20 years.

M.A.S.L.

Years of Experience
About IceDrive

ICE TRACK
SINCE THE 1990s

Since the mid-1990s, Golsfjellet has been the base for ice track driving. In 2015 the plan opened approximately 1930 acres at Tisleifjorden.

A professional facility for staff training, vehicle launches, and emergency vehicle training under demanding winter conditions.

No vehicle moves onto the ice without documented safe thickness. Daily drill measurements — radar surveys twice per season.

50%
Professional
Car dealers & tyre companies
40%
Emergency
Police & ambulance
10%
Private
Car clubs & companies
Training & Regulations

BEFORE YOU DRIVE

5m
Depot spacing
100m
On track
40km/h
Service road
82dB
Noise Mon–Fri
01

Driver Requirements

  • Valid driving licence carried at all times
  • Zero tolerance: alcohol, narcotics, medication
  • Traffic rules apply for drivers and pedestrians
  • Seatbelt mandatory during all driving
  • Observe speed limits to and from the track
02

Vehicle Requirements

  • Road-registered vehicles only — written consent otherwise
  • ATV/UTV: prior written approval required
  • No oil or fuel leaks on the ice
  • No repairs carried out on the ice
  • Standard exhausts meet noise requirements
03

Driving Rules

  • 5m between cars in depot — 10m in January
  • ~100m between cars on track at all times
  • Driver change in depot only, never on track
  • Max 40 km/h on service roads between tracks
  • One-way — overtaking only when signalled
04

Towing Assistance

  • Attach tow hook BEFORE starting — always
  • If stuck: activate hazard lights immediately
  • Driver and passenger remain in the vehicle
  • Driver attaches/removes tow strap themselves
  • Reduce to 20 km/h when passing tractor
Tyre TypeStatusCondition
Standard winter tyres✔ Always—
Studded tyres (road)✔ PermittedRoad-approved
Lappi tyres◎ ConditionalMax 2.5mm · Written application
Rally stud / Ice wheels✘ ForbiddenCar removed
⚠️ Insurance: Standard comprehensive insurance does NOT cover track damage. Check with your insurer and consider specialist track cover.
Infrastructure

THE TRACKS

Ice Track Map 2025 · Tisleifjorden
Q1
Quattro-1Rhythmic circuit. Tight bends, straight and private depot.
2,32 km
Q2
Quattro-2Extended Quattro variant with test plate and snake section.
2,43 km
SK
SkogshornTechnical short track. Precision demands.
0,75 km
VH
VeslehornLarge track with snake, test plate and depot/WC.
2,97 km
FH
Fanitullen HighspeedHigh-speed with serpentine plate and drift section.
1,70 km
FT
Fanitullen TeknikkTechnical track with braking/evasion and drift.
2,05 km
RB
RundbaneLongest circuit. Bandy rink, test plate, drift section.
3,68 km
FAQ

QUESTIONS & ANSWERS

How is safety managed?+
Daily ice thickness drilling and logging. Radar surveys twice per season. Track supervisor always present — 6m depot spacing, 100m on circuit. Full tow assistance on standby.
Which tyres are permitted?+
Standard winter and studded tyres always allowed. Lappi tyres (max 2.5mm) with written application. Rally studs and ice wheels are forbidden — cars removed from track.
How is the ice prepared?+
Tracks cut with a rotary cutter each evening. Even, consistent grip. Entire area prepared by snowmobile from early December.
What about insurance?+
Standard comprehensive insurance does NOT cover track damage. Check with your insurer and consider specialist track cover.
What noise limits apply?+
82 dB(A) Mon–Fri, 79 dB(A) Saturday — measured 10m from track edge. Standard exhausts typically comply.
Saturday break required?+
Yes — one-hour mandatory break at Saturday events. Timing coordinated by IceDrive so all activity stops simultaneously.
Gallery

ON THE ICE

Accommodation

WHERE TO STAY

⏱ 3 min to track
Golsfjellet Fjellstue
Closest. 965m. Rally loft with Solberg trophies. ATV and snowmobile.
+47 909 83 058
⏱ 10 min to track
Oset Fjellhotell
Family-run mountain hotel. Fine dining and swimming pool.
+47 32 07 95 00
🏔
⏱ 15 min to track
Storefjellstølen
Cabin rentals at 1028m. Outstanding views.
+47 976 88 000
⏱ 20 min to track
Storefjell Resort
Full-service resort. Skiing, pool and concerts.
+47 32 07 80 00
⏱ 25 min to track
Pers Hotel, Gol
Tropicana waterpark, SPA and bowling.
+47 32 02 30 00

Why IceDrive?
NORWAY'S
FINEST

No vehicle moves onto the ice without documented safe thickness. Daily drill measurements and logging — radar surveys twice per season.

Daily prep with a rotary cutter each evening. Even, consistent grip for every session.

The Place
Norway.
Frozen.
Untamed.

Tisleifjorden, Golsfjellet. 900 metres above sea level.

Norway's largest ice circuit — active since the mid-1990s.

Used year after year by those who understand what this terrain demands.

Tisleifjorden · Golsfjellet · By appointment only
50%
Professional
Car dealers, tyre companies & schools
40%
Emergency Services
Police and ambulance training
10%
Private Groups
Car clubs & corporate events
Safety & Operations
Controlled.
Supervised.
Non-negotiable.

Every event starts with a mandatory driver briefing. All participants sign a declaration. These regulations apply to everyone without exception.

IceDrive has recovery vehicles and tractors on standby at all times.

5 m
Depot spacing
100 m
On track
40 km/h
Service road
82 dB
Noise Mon–Fri

  • Valid driving licence carried at all times
  • Zero tolerance: alcohol, narcotics, medication

  • Seatbelt mandatory during all driving
  • Observe speed limits to and from the track
02

  • Road-registered vehicles only — written consent otherwise
  • ATV/UTV: prior written approval required
  • No oil or fuel leaks on the ice
  • No repairs carried out on the ice
  • Standard exhausts meet noise requirements
03

Driving Rules

  • 5m between cars in depot — 10m in January
  • ~100m between cars on track at all times
  • Driver change in depot only, never on track
  • Max 40 km/h on service roads between tracks
  • One-way — overtaking only when signalled
04

Towing Assistance

  • Attach tow hook BEFORE starting — always
  • If stuck: activate hazard lights immediately
  • Driver and passenger remain in the vehicle
  • Driver attaches/removes tow strap themselves
  • Reduce to 20 km/h when passing tractor

Questions & Answers
Frequently asked questions
How is safety managed?+
Daily ice thickness drilling and logging. Radar surveys twice per season. Track supervisor always present — 6m depot spacing, 100m on circuit. Full tow assistance on standby.
Which tyres are permitted?+
Standard winter and studded tyres always allowed. Lappi tyres (max 2.5mm) with written application. Rally studs and ice wheels are forbidden — cars removed from track.
How is the ice prepared?+
Tracks cut with a rotary cutter each evening. Even, consistent grip. Entire area prepared by snowmobile from early December.
What about insurance?+
Standard comprehensive insurance does NOT cover track damage. Check with your insurer and consider specialist track cover.
What noise limits apply?+
82 dB(A) Mon–Fri, 79 dB(A) Saturday — measured 10m from track edge. Standard exhausts typically comply.
Saturday break required?+
Yes — one-hour mandatory break at Saturday events. Timing coordinated by IceDrive so all activity stops simultaneously.

About IceDrive
The story behind the ice

Since the mid-1990s, Golsfjellet has been the base for ice track driving. In 2015 the plan opened approximately 1930 acres at Tisleifjorden.

A professional facility used for staff training, vehicle launches, and emergency services training under demanding winter conditions.

No vehicle moves onto the ice without documented safe thickness. Daily drill measurements — radar surveys twice per season.

Professional clients50%
Emergency services40%
Private groups & companies10%

  • Traffic rules apply for drivers and pedestrians
  • Seatbelt mandatory during all driving
  • Observe speed limits to and from the track

  • Road-registered vehicles only — written consent otherwise
  • ATV/UTV: prior written approval required
  • No oil or fuel leaks on the ice
  • No repairs carried out on the ice
  • Standard exhausts meet noise requirements
03

Driving Rules

  • 5m between cars in depot — 10m in January
  • ~100m between cars on track at all times
  • Driver change in depot only, never on track
  • Max 40 km/h on service roads between tracks
  • One-way — overtaking only when signalled
04

Towing Assistance

  • Attach tow hook BEFORE starting — always
  • If stuck: activate hazard lights immediately
  • Driver and passenger remain in the vehicle
  • Driver attaches/removes tow strap themselves
  • Reduce to 20 km/h when passing tractor
